steps.title: Set y equals zero and identify all roots
steps.content:
To find x-intercepts, set $y = 0$:
$$3(x-k)(x+k)(x+r)(x-k) = 0$$
Since 3 is nonzero, each factor can equal zero:
  • $(x-k) = 0 \Rightarrow x = k$ (appears with multiplicity 2)
  • $(x+k) = 0 \Rightarrow x = -k$
  • $(x+r) = 0 \Rightarrow x = -r$
When counting distinct x-intercepts, we count each distinct $x$-value once, regardless of multiplicity.

steps.title: Verify all roots are distinct
steps.content:
Are the three values $k$, $-k$, and $-r$ all different? Given $k \gt 0$, $r \gt 0$, and $k eq r$:
  • $k \gt 0$ while $-k \lt 0$, so $k eq -k$
  • $k \gt 0$ while $-r \lt 0$, so $k eq -r$
  • Since $k eq r$, we have $-k eq -r$
All three roots are distinct, so there are 3 distinct x-intercepts. That confirms B is correct.
